All conversions between smaller units and larger units follow the same place value pattern. Observe how the decimal moves based on the unit relationship.
You don't need to memorize every table if you remember the logic of zeros. The number of zeros in the conversion factor tells you the number of decimal places:

children.text: Train C has the smallest delay. Paise is
Train A has the largest delay.

children.text: Train B is delayed more than Train A.
4.5 hours is the same as 4 hours 5 minutes.
Correct! 4.5 (or 4.50) is the largest delay, and 4.05 is the smallest.
